problem_tags: failure:partial; checklist:request_capacity; issue:eta_health; decision:user_pending; rate_limit:bitstream_429; throughput:file_lane_single_stream; eta:over_48h; scope:completeness_vs_timeline_tradeoff
changes_to_try: If full coverage is still required, run a longer low-frequency file probe that spaces requests well beyond 10 seconds, records reset headers for every attempt, and recomputes ETA from the observed sustainable cadence before starting download work.; Keep metadata discovery on the proven machine-readable surfaces and split it from file delivery so the file lane can stay at one conservative stream with strict 429 backoff and exact throughput logging.; If the user wants a faster path, narrow the first crawl to the 10,029-item masters-thesis collections plus 363 doctoral items and defer scan-heavy collections such as Σπάνιες εκδόσεις and Παλαιά Συγγράμματα.
improvement_hypotheses: A longer backend cooldown than the visible front-end reset window is governing bitstream delivery, so a sparse timed probe could still establish a safe sustained cadence.; Separating metadata enumeration from file delivery, using REST or OAI-PMH for metadata and a single throttled file lane for PDFs, is the most plausible way to preserve completeness without increasing 429 risk.; If the user prefers time-bounded value over whole-repository completeness, the eight departmental masters-thesis collections plus the doctoral lane (10,392 items total) are the best Greek-text-first scope reduction.
